sources.code:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head: ListNode) -> ListNode:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        prev, curr = dummy, head

        while curr and curr.next:
            # save ptrs
            nxtPair = curr.next.next
            second = curr.next

            # reverse this pair
            second.next = curr
            curr.next = nxtPair
            prev.next = second

            # update ptrs
            prev = curr
            curr = nxtPair

        return dummy.next

# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head: ListNode) -> ListNode:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        prev, curr = dummy, head

        while curr and curr.next:
            # save ptrs
            nxtPair = curr.next.next
            second = curr.next

            # reverse this pair
            second.next = curr
            curr.next = nxtPair
            prev.next = second

            # update ptrs
            prev = curr
            curr = nxtPair

        return dummy.next

class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head: ListNode) -> ListNode:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        prev, curr = dummy, head

            prev = curr
            curr = nxtPair

sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

class ListNode(object):
    # [Reader note] Entry point
    # Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Treat each pair as a local surgery while preserving the handle to everything not yet processed.
    def __init__(self, x):
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Temporary references, local rewiring order, and preserving access to the unprocessed remainder of the list.
        self.val = x
        self.next = None

    def __repr__(self):
        if self:
            return "{} -> {}".format(self.val, self.next)

class Solution(object):
    # @param a ListNode
    # @return a ListNode
    def swapPairs(self, head):
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head
        current = dummy
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        while current.next and current.next.next:
            next_one, next_two, next_three = current.next, current.next.next, current.next.next.next
            current.next = next_two
            next_two.next = next_one
            next_one.next = next_three
            current = next_one
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return dummy.next

sources.code:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        if head is None or head.next is None:
            return head
        t = self.swapPairs(head.next.next)
        p = head.next
        p.next = head
        head.next = t
        return p
sources.originalCode:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        if head is None or head.next is None:
            return head
        t = self.swapPairs(head.next.next)
        p = head.next
        p.next = head
        head.next = t
        return p
sources.reviewedCode:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Treat each pair as a local surgery while preserving the handle to everything not yet processed.
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
# [Reader note] State setup
# This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Temporary references, local rewiring order, and preserving access to the unprocessed remainder of the list.
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def swapPairs(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        # [Reader note] Decision point
        # Pay attention here: If you change links in the wrong order, you can lose the rest of the list almost immediately.
        if head is None or head.next is None:
            return head
        t = self.swapPairs(head.next.next)
        p = head.next
        p.next = head
        head.next = t
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return p

writeups.excerpt: ## Prerequisites Before attempting this problem, you should be comfortable with: - **Linked List Fundamentals** - Understanding node structure, traversal, and pointer manipulation in singly linked lists - **In-Place Pointer Manipulation** - Reversing or reordering nodes by changing next pointers without extra data structures - **Dummy Node Technique** - Using a sentinel node to simplify edge cases when the head of t...
